prompt: Explain the water cycle, in prose, without using any lists, bullets, headings, or numbered steps. Keep it to one paragraph.
response: The water cycle is a continuous process that replenishes water on Earth. It begins with evaporation, when the sun heats water in rivers, lakes, and oceans, turning it into water vapor that rises into the air. This water vapor forms clouds through condensation. When the conditions are right, this water falls back to the ground as precipitation - in the form of rain, snow, sleet, or hail. Much of this water flows into streams and rivers, and ultimately back into the oceans, where it can evaporate again. Some of it, however, seeps into the ground to become groundwater. This groundwater sustains plants and can also feed back into rivers and oceans over time.
